title: The One with the Prom Video

jokes.text: Well, thanks, man. Now I can get my pony.
jokes.setup: Joey pays Chandler back money he owes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry response about finally affording a pony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan delivery with absurdist twist. Economy of words with strong character voice.

jokes.text: Thanks, best bud.
jokes.setup: Joey gives Chandler an engraved bracelet calling him 'best bud'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's forced enthusiasm reading the cheesy engraving
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bracelet storyline effectively, mild cringe humor from the awkward gift situation.

jokes.text: Well, I've never been good to go, so... What the hell, why not try some jewelry.
jokes.setup: Joey insists the bracelet will make Chandler 'good to go'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware admission he's never been good to go
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's self-deprecating nature and reluctant acceptance.

jokes.text: Any idea what this will do for your sex life? It might slow down at first, but... once I'm used to the extra weight, I'll be back on track.
jokes.setup: Joey asks about the bracelet's effect on Chandler's sex life
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats it like athletic equipment affecting performance
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection treating jewelry as athletic gear, with perfect Chandler logic.

jokes.text: Is it dirty? Oh, I'm gonna wash it. Don't. I like it dirty.
jokes.setup: Monica making salad during job interview, manager asks about lettuce
jokes.punchline: Manager's inappropriate 'I like it dirty' comment
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo that relies on sexual suggestion without clever setup or character specificity.

jokes.text: Are they firm? They're okay. You sure they haven't gone bad? You sure they're not very, very bad?
jokes.setup: Manager continuing inappropriate questions about food preparation
jokes.punchline: Escalating sexual innuendo with 'very, very bad'
jokes.explanation: Continues the cheap innuendo pattern, escalation without cleverness.

jokes.text: I'm out of here!
jokes.setup: Manager's increasingly inappropriate behavior during interview
jokes.punchline: Monica's sudden exit from the uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the uncomfortable scene, Monica's decisive reaction feels earned.

jokes.text: Is it like 'at the bat' or 'and The Sunshine Band'?
jokes.setup: Taking message for Rachel from someone named Casey
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's reference to Casey Kasem vs KC and the Sunshine Band
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe moment showing her quirky logic and pop culture confusion in character-specific way.

jokes.text: I guess he wants to do a little dance... you know, make a little love... Well, get down tonight.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what Casey wants with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe singing KC and the Sunshine Band lyrics as answer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that turns the name confusion into song lyrics, perfectly Phoebe behavior.

jokes.text: Because she's your lobster.
jokes.setup: Ross despairing about Rachel dating other people
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarre but confident assertion about lobsters
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends moment. Perfect Phoebe logic, became cultural reference. Economy, originality, and character integration all exceptional.

jokes.text: You know what? You can actually see old lobster couples... walking around their tank, holding claws, like...
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining lobster mating behavior
jokes.punchline: Phoebe presumably mimicking lobster claw-holding
jokes.explanation: Extension of the lobster concept with physical comedy element, maintains the absurdist logic.

jokes.text: You have to picture lobsters.
jokes.setup: After demonstrating lobster behavior
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact instruction to imagine lobsters
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery, acknowledges the absurdity while maintaining Phoebe's commitment to her logic.

jokes.text: Yeah, I think that fifth shower got the interview off me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Monica feels better after bad interview
jokes.punchline: Monica claiming it took five showers to feel clean
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the uncomfortable interview with relatable exaggeration.

jokes.text: There is the possibility that I won't make rent.
jokes.setup: Asked about other job possibilities
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry acknowledgment of potential homelessness
jokes.explanation: Standard deadpan response to financial stress, competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You feel guilty and tense around them already. Might as well make some money off of them.
jokes.setup: Monica worrying about borrowing from parents vs friends
jokes.punchline: Ross's pragmatic observation about family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about family relationships with practical logic.

jokes.text: No, no. It's not flashy. Not for a goodfella.
jokes.setup: Friends commenting on his flashy bracelet
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reference to being a 'goodfella'
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm deflecting embarrassment about the gaudy bracelet.

jokes.text: Man, that is sharp! That must have cost you quite a few doubloons.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Chandler's expensive-looking bracelet
jokes.punchline: Ross using 'doubloons' as currency reference
jokes.explanation: Quirky word choice that fits the pirate/treasure theme, mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: No, it's fun, it's fun. It's kinda like knowing one of the Gabor Sisters.
jokes.setup: Defending giving Chandler the flashy bracelet
jokes.punchline: Joey's comparison to knowing glamorous Hungarian actresses
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that shows Joey's aspirational mindset but relies on name-dropping.

jokes.text: Wow, that is a surprise! Just one question. Why not Ross's room?
jokes.setup: Parents announcing they're turning her room into a gym
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic enthusiasm followed by practical question
jokes.explanation: Good Monica reaction showing her competitive nature with Ross and dry wit.

jokes.text: We didn't want to disturb them. God forbid!
jokes.setup: Parents explaining Ross has too many trophies to move
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter 'God forbid' about disturbing Ross's achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Monica's sibling rivalry and resentment toward parental favoritism.

jokes.text: Did I mention I still don't have a boyfriend? Well, I just assumed.
jokes.setup: Parents asking what's new with Monica
jokes.punchline: Mother's casual assumption Monica is still single
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing showing the mother's casual cruelty and family dysfunction dynamics.

jokes.text: I can't say any of us were surprised.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Chandler's parents' separation
jokes.punchline: Father's blunt admission they saw it coming
jokes.explanation: Typical blunt parent comment, shows family's lack of tact but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What? What incident? No, no. I must be thinking of someone else. Maybe me!
jokes.setup: Mother mentions mysterious Hawaii incident
jokes.punchline: Father's awkward backpedaling and self-incrimination
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with escalating awkwardness, shows parents' dysfunction.

jokes.text: Don't you have some folding to do? Fold. You fold.
jokes.setup: Father almost revealing embarrassing information
jokes.punchline: Mother's desperate attempt to distract him with busywork
jokes.explanation: Shows mother's controlling nature and desperation to change subject, functional humor.

jokes.text: That was really good. I think I'm ready for my penis now.
jokes.setup: Chandler practicing pickup lines at coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually ordering food with unfortunate name
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - audience thinks pickup line practice, revealed as innocent food order.

jokes.text: I know what you're thinking. Dave Thomas, founder of Wendy's.
jokes.setup: Woman approaching Chandler after his embarrassing moment
jokes.punchline: She thinks he looks like Wendy's founder
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference that doesn't add much, feels like filler.

jokes.text: He could have gotten me a VCR or a set of golf clubs. But no! He got me the 'woman repeller'!
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about the bracelet gift
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of it as a 'woman repeller'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's frustration with the bracelet, character-appropriate dramatics.

jokes.text: The eyesore from the Liberace House of Crap!
jokes.setup: Continuing rant about the bracelet
jokes.punchline: Chandler's over-the-top description mixing Liberace with 'house of crap'
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor combining gaudy performer with crude description, fits Chandler's style.

jokes.text: 'I pity the fool that puts on my jewelry! I do! I do!' 'I pity that fool...'
jokes.setup: Chandler comparing bracelet to Mr. T style
jokes.punchline: Both doing Mr. T impressions about the jewelry
jokes.explanation: Standard impression humor, competent but relies on easy pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Do your Marcel Marceau.
jokes.setup: Joey interrupting the Mr. T impressions
jokes.punchline: Asking for mime performance (presumably silent)
jokes.explanation: Joey's oblivious interruption with unexpected request shows his character well.

jokes.text: It's like, back off, buddy! She's a waitress, not a geisha.
jokes.setup: Ross getting jealous watching man talk to waitress
jokes.punchline: His dramatic comparison to geisha expectations
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's jealous overreaction with unexpected cultural comparison.

jokes.text: Ross Geller. God, in your ad you said you were pretty, but wow!
jokes.setup: Ross approaching woman at coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Pretending to be responding to personal ad to interrupt conversation
jokes.explanation: Clever improvised interruption showing Ross's desperation and quick thinking.

jokes.text: You're welcome.
jokes.setup: After successfully ruining Rachel's conversation
jokes.punchline: Ross's self-satisfied response when he wasn't helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross obliviousness, thinking he helped when he interfered.

jokes.text: Saving me from a pleasant conversation with an interesting man?
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he was 'saving' Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sharp retort about what he actually interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good comeback line showing Rachel's frustration with Ross's interference.

jokes.text: You're my lobster.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to explain his behavior to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross using Phoebe's lobster theory inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Important callback moment, shows Ross desperately grasping at Phoebe's logic.

jokes.text: In the tank! You know, with... with the holding and... Pheebs, you wanna help me with the lobster thing?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to explain lobster theory to confused Rachel
jokes.punchline: His frustrated inability to articulate it and call for backup
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's desperation, shows him failing to communicate the concept.

jokes.text: Do the claws again.
jokes.setup: Ross asking for help with lobster explanation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unhelpful focus on the physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response, focused on wrong aspect while Ross is desperate.

jokes.text: Except that what?
jokes.setup: Ross saying 'accept that' about them never happening
jokes.punchline: Rachel mishearing 'accept' as 'except'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay moment that creates brief confusion and hope for Ross.

jokes.text: You're getting good at that Marcel Marceau thing.
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Joey silent treatment over bracelet
jokes.punchline: Joey's oblivious compliment about the mime act
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Joey's obliviousness to Chandler's anger, thinking it's performance.

jokes.text: Doesn't the fact that I wore it... say how much our friendship means to me?
jokes.setup: Joey defending his hurt feelings about Chandler's reaction
jokes.punchline: His sincere belief that wearing unwanted gift shows friendship
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's sweet but misguided logic about friendship gestures.

jokes.text: Well, that's the part where I'm a wank.
jokes.setup: Joey acknowledging he insulted the bracelet and made fun of Chandler
jokes.punchline: His casual admission using British slang
jokes.explanation: Joey's unexpected self-awareness with odd word choice, shows character growth.

jokes.text: I am here, on my knees... holding up these couch cushions... as a symbol of my sorrow and regret. Much like they did in biblical times.
jokes.setup: Joey making dramatic apology to Chandler
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous biblical comparison with couch cushions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey moment combining his dramatic tendencies with absurd historical comparison.

jokes.text: Though you may haveth anger now...
jokes.setup: Joey continuing biblical-style apology
jokes.punchline: His attempt at old-timey language with 'haveth'
jokes.explanation: Extension of the biblical bit showing Joey's poor grasp of archaic language.

jokes.text: You know, that Steffi Graf has quite a tush. I'm just saying! It's right there!
jokes.setup: Father watching tennis on TV
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate commentary about tennis player
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate inappropriate dad comment but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A woman in my office is a lesbian. I'm just saying!
jokes.setup: Casual family conversation about Ross's custody situation
jokes.punchline: Father's completely random lesbian announcement
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's tendency toward inappropriate random comments, builds character consistency.

jokes.text: Judy, relax. This is our little Harmonica we're talking about.
jokes.setup: Mother worried about Monica losing her job
jokes.punchline: Father calling Monica 'Harmonica' instead of her name
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Geller moment showing his endearing confusion and creating memorable nickname.

jokes.text: Get out of the bitter barn and play in the hay.
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about replacing the expensive bracelet
jokes.punchline: Ross's folksy advice with farm metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative folk wisdom metaphor that's surprisingly wise and quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, now you have two!
jokes.setup: Gunther finding Chandler's lost bracelet after he bought replacement
jokes.punchline: Gunther's oblivious enthusiasm about the 'good news'
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for maximum irony, Gunther's obliviousness to Chandler's dismay.

jokes.text: Check it out! We're bracelet buddies! That's what they'll call us!
jokes.setup: Joey giving Chandler the second bracelet as friendship gesture
jokes.punchline: Joey's excited invention of 'bracelet buddies' nickname
jokes.explanation: Sweet Joey moment showing his genuine friendship enthusiasm, creates memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Hey, you're a cheap-a-saurus!
jokes.setup: Monica noting Ross has dinosaur checks
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive dinosaur pun about Monica being cheap
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross dinosaur obsession turned into defensive joke, good character integration.

jokes.text: I thought they used that to cover Connecticut when it rains.
jokes.setup: Monica showing her large high school bathing suit
jokes.punchline: Ross's exaggerated size comparison to covering a state
jokes.explanation: Good sibling teasing with creative exaggeration, visual humor callback later.

jokes.text: They had to reduce it because of my deviated septum.
jokes.setup: Friends shocked by Monica's large nose in old video
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Monica's nose storyline with practical explanation.

jokes.text: Okay, I was wrong. That's what they use to cover Connecticut.
jokes.setup: Seeing Monica's actual nose size in the video
jokes.punchline: Ross upgrading his bathing suit joke to the nose
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and escalation, Ross admits the nose is even bigger than the bathing suit.

jokes.text: Some girl ate Monica!
jokes.setup: Seeing how much larger Monica was in high school
jokes.punchline: Ross's shocked reaction treating it like someone else entirely
jokes.explanation: Creative way to express shock at dramatic weight difference, strong visual comedy.

jokes.text: So how many cameras are actually on you?
jokes.setup: Monica claiming camera adds ten pounds
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mathematical extension of her excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler mathematical humor, takes common excuse to logical extreme with devastating effect.

jokes.text: Looking good, Mr. Kotter.
jokes.setup: Ross in his prom tuxedo in the video
jokes.punchline: Date's reference to Welcome Back Kotter TV show
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference without much cleverness or setup.

jokes.text: You know, Roy saw Star Wars 317 times. He was in the paper.
jokes.setup: Friends watching prom video, seeing Roy Gublik
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact statement about Roy's obsessive movie-watching record
jokes.explanation: Good period detail showing 80s movie obsession culture, sets up character types.

jokes.text: Take a chill pill.
jokes.setup: Rachel panicking about Chip not arriving for prom
jokes.punchline: Friend using distinctly 80s slang to calm her down
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate slang that establishes the 80s setting but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I just told Rachel that Roy touched my boob.
jokes.setup: Monica whispering to her friend in the prom video
jokes.punchline: Her teenage gossip revelation
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage behavior that shows young Monica's gossipy nature.

jokes.text: My! Are you handsome!
jokes.setup: Ross appearing in tuxedo ready to take Rachel to prom
jokes.punchline: Mother's enthusiastic compliment to Ross (vs her treatment of Monica)
jokes.explanation: Reinforces established pattern of parents favoring Ross over Monica.

jokes.text: Rachel, ready or not, here comes your knight in shining...
jokes.setup: Young Ross approaching dramatically to ask Rachel to prom
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-speech by Chip's arrival
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for romantic gesture getting interrupted, shows Ross's theatrical nature.

jokes.text: Oh, dear! How do I turn this off? Press the button. Which one? Which button? Jack!
jokes.setup: Parents trying to turn off video camera after awkward moment
jokes.punchline: Escalating confusion about technology
jokes.explanation: Standard older people vs technology humor, competent but predictable.

jokes.text: I can't believe you did that.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizing Ross was willing to take her to prom when Chip stood her up
jokes.punchline: Her emotional reaction to his past kindness
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff moment that recontextualizes Ross's feelings, sets up romantic resolution.

jokes.text: See? He's her lobster!
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel finally kissing after watching the prom video
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's vindicated declaration about her lobster theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the lobster theory, Phoebe's moment of triumph. Iconic line that validates her absurd logic.

jokes.text: I don't know any of your flashdances, but I'm no slouch on the dance floor.
jokes.setup: Monica asking her father to dance
jokes.punchline: Jack's confused reference to 'flashdances' as a dance type
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Geller confusion mixing up movie title with dance style, endearing character moment.

tmdb_overview: Joey pays back Chandler after landing his Days role. Monica struggles financially. A prom video reveals Monica's past weight and Rachel's former nose and Ross's long-standing feelings.
